plan_type_rationale: Light plan: new standalone Python file (tools/coderef_ui.py), no changes to existing coderef-core src/. Zero blast radius into production code.

tasks.description: Create tools/coderef_ui.py — tkinter GUI wrapping all 15 coderef-core CLI bins. Implement: project dir picker (persists to .coderef-ui-prefs.json), command selector grouped by category (Pipeline, Scan/Populate, RAG, Query/Analyze, Utilities), dynamic options panel that swaps widgets per command (dropdowns for enums, checkboxes for booleans, text entries for free-form args, fields hidden when irrelevant), color-coded status badge (Idle/Running/Done/Error), Run/Stop/Clear buttons, scrollable streaming output pane (monospace, auto-scroll). Use threading.Thread + subprocess.Popen for non-blocking execution. See analysis.json per_command_flags for the full flag map per command.
acceptance: File is runnable: python tools/coderef_ui.py opens the window
All 15 commands present in selector, grouped by category
Options panel swaps correctly when switching commands
Run button spawns subprocess and streams output to pane
Status badge updates: Idle → Running → Done/Error
Query/target fields visible only for relevant commands
Last project dir persists across restarts via .coderef-ui-prefs.json

tasks.description: Smoke-test the UI manually: launch with python tools/coderef_ui.py, select a project dir, run coderef-pipeline with --dry-run or rag-status (fast), confirm output streams, status badge updates, Stop works.
acceptance: At least one command runs and produces output

tasks.description: Commit tools/coderef_ui.py with message: feat(tools): add tkinter control panel for coderef-core CLI

ac: All 15 CLI commands accessible from UI, grouped by category
Status badge reflects run state correctly
No changes to src/ TypeScript files
